claims.text: Different objects in space — comets, planets, moons, gas clouds — each have their own distinctive aroma.

evidence.quote:
Objects in space like
comets planets moons gas clouds.
Each have their own distinctive aroma.

claims.rationale: Strictly, 'aroma' requires a medium and a nose — vacuum has neither. But the episode means chemical signatures that could be smelled in a pressurized atmosphere on-site, which is a reasonable generalization.

claims.text: Apollo 17 astronaut Jack Schmitt said moon dust smelled to everyone like spent gunpowder.

evidence.quote:
Apollo
17 Jack Schmidt said everyone's impression of
the smell was that of spent gunpowder.

claims.rationale: Schmitt (Apollo 17 LM pilot) is on record describing lunar dust as smelling like spent gunpowder after his EVAs — corroborated by other Apollo astronauts including Cernan, Aldrin.

claims.text: Lunar regolith is chemically unlike modern gunpowder despite smelling similar: regolith is silicon-dioxide glass from billions of years of meteorite impacts, while gunpowder is nitrocellulose and nitroglycerin.

evidence.quote:
Moon Regolith is made up of silicon
dioxide glass created by billions of years
of media impacts.

modern gunpowder is a combination
of nitro cellulose and nitroglycerin.

claims.rationale: Lunar regolith is dominantly impact-shocked silicates (agglutinate glass). Modern smokeless powders are indeed nitrocellulose/nitroglycerin. The composition mismatch is correct, which is precisely why the gunpowder-smell analogy is scientifically interesting.

claims.text: No one actually knows why moon dust smells like gunpowder.
claims.rationale: Consensus science: the gunpowder smell of lunar regolith has no definitive mechanism. Leading hypotheses are solar-wind-implanted volatiles and reactive dust surfaces oxidizing on contact with cabin humidity.

claims.text: Once lunar dust samples returned to Earth they were completely odorless.

evidence.quote:
By the time the moon dust made it
back to Earth it was completely odorless.

claims.rationale: True — lunar samples brought back to Earth do not smell. The transient smell in the cabin is the key evidence for the 'activated on exposure' hypothesis.

claims.text: Astronauts returning from spacewalks have reported smelling burnt metal or burnt meat on their spacesuits.

evidence.quote:
Astronauts returning from spacewalks have reported smelling
burnt metal or burnt meat

claims.rationale: ISS astronauts (Don Pettit, Scott Kelly, Thomas Jones, others) have repeatedly described the 'smell of space' after EVAs as seared steak, welding fumes, or hot metal.

claims.text: Venus's thick CO2 atmosphere and sulfuric-acid clouds make it smell like a burnt match and rotten eggs.

evidence.quote:
That combination makes Venus smell like
a burnt match and rotten eggs.

claims.rationale: Venus's atmosphere is ~96% CO2 with sulfuric acid clouds and some SO2. 'Burnt match' (SO2) and 'rotten eggs' (H2S) are chemically plausible descriptors. But the surface pressure (~90 atm) means any human nose would be destroyed before smelling anything.

claims.text: Mars lost its magnetosphere billions of years ago, letting solar winds strip away its atmosphere and leaving a thin iron-oxide-rich environment.

evidence.quote:
Mars is often referred to as the red planet
because of the iron oxide on the surface.

Mars lost its magnetic sphere
billions of years ago.

claims.rationale: Established planetary science: Mars's core dynamo stopped ~4 Gyr ago, and MAVEN confirmed solar wind stripping as the mechanism for atmospheric loss. Iron-oxide (hematite) surface is confirmed by Pathfinder, Spirit, Opportunity spectra.

claims.text: Jupiter's outer atmospheric layers are ammonia-rich, giving them a horse-urine smell.

evidence.quote:
The outer layers have a lot of
ammonia which smells like horse urine.

claims.rationale: Jupiter's upper atmosphere does contain ammonia clouds (Juno confirmed NH3 banding). Ammonia indeed has the horse-urine smell. Fair chemistry-to-scent mapping.

claims.text: Saturn's atmosphere is 96% hydrogen and 4% helium with traces of methane, ammonia, phosphine and acetylene.

evidence.quote:
Saturn's atmosphere is 96 percent hydrogen and 4
percent helium

claims.rationale: Cassini and modern remote-sensing measurements confirm Saturn's atmosphere at approximately 96% H2, 3% He, with traces of the listed hydrocarbons and phosphine.

claims.text: Uranus's upper cloud tops are made of hydrogen sulfide, the compound that causes the rotten-egg smell.

evidence.quote:
the cloud tops of Uranus are made
of hydrogen sulfide

claims.rationale: Gemini North near-infrared spectroscopy in 2018 (Irwin et al., Nature Astronomy) directly detected H2S in Uranus's cloud tops — the stinkbomb result. Broadly accepted.

claims.text: Neptune has essentially no smell because its chemistry is mostly frozen out of the gas phase.

evidence.quote:
Neptune doesn't have
much of a smell at all.

claims.rationale: Neptune does have H2S, methane, ammonia like Uranus. Framing it as scentless is a rhetorical choice — it would smell, just less because of lower atmospheric temperature condensing smelly molecules out.

claims.text: Saturn's moon Enceladus vents methanol plumes from its subsurface ocean, so it could smell like a martini.

evidence.quote:
plumes of methanol
are ejected from the surface so it
could smell like a martini.

claims.rationale: Enceladus plumes were recently detected to contain methanol (Herschel 2011, Cassini mass-spec analyses). 'Martini' is cheeky — methanol is not the main aroma of a martini (that's vermouth/gin botanicals, + ethanol).

claims.text: Scientists discovered ethyl formate in the galactic-center dust cloud Sagittarius B2 — the compound responsible for raspberry flavor and rum scent.

evidence.quote:
scientists have discovered a substance called ethyl
for me which is the chemical
responsible for the flavor of raspberries
and the smell of rum.

claims.rationale: 2009 radio-astronomy detection of ethyl formate in Sgr B2 by Belloche et al. is the real basis for this frequently-cited fun fact.

claims.text: Despite the premise, the moon landings were real and were not faked.

evidence.quote:
We are going to assume
the moon landing was real.

claims.rationale: Host explicitly rejects moon-landing-hoax framing as a prerequisite for the episode. Apollo landings are corroborated by retroreflectors still pinged by Earth-based lasers, Soviet confirmation, and hundreds of kg of samples.
